customTitle: code:Fix card.js null input rendering as 'null' string

agentName: code:Fix card.js null input rendering as 'null' string

content:
# Task: Fix card.js null input rendering as 'null' string ## Description ## Overview ## Problem `createCard({ title: null, body: null })` sets `h2.textContent = null` which produces the visible string `"null"` in the DOM — not an empty string. JavaScript destructuring defaults (`title = ''`) only trigger for `undefined`, not `null`. This causes: 1. A broken test at `src/components/card.test.js:43-47` that asserts `toBe('')` but would actually receive `"null"` 2. Surprising visible rendering if any caller passes `null` explicitly ## Fix In `src/components/card.js`, change lines 13 and 16 to use nullish coalescing: ```js // Before h2.textContent = title // ... p.textContent = body // After h2.textContent = title ?? '' // ... p.textContent = body ?? '' ``` The destructuring defaults on line 8 (`title = ''`, `body = ''`) should remain — they handle `undefined`. The `?? ''` handles `null` at point of use. Together they cover both cases. ## Acceptance Criteria - `createCard({ title: null, body: null })` renders empty `

` and `

` (not the string `"null"`) - `createCard()` and `createCard({ title: undefined })` still render empty strings - The existing test `'sets empty string for null title and body'` in `card.test.js:43-47` passes - All other card tests continue to pass - No changes needed to `card.test.js` — the test's expectations are already correct ## Context - **Category:** bugfix - **Priority Score:** 7/10 - **Estimated Complexity:** low ## Reasoning The null-bypass of destructuring defaults is a real JS gotcha that produces visibly wrong output ('null' rendered in the DOM) and a broken test. Two-character fix per field in card.js. Verified by running node -e to confirm String(null) === 'null' and null bypasses JS destructuring defaults. ## Category: bugfix ## Context - Product: RSI Starter: Vanilla JS + Vite - Purpose: Starter template maintained by RSI for new product scaffolding - Deployed path: /opt/rsi-platform/repos/starter-vanilla-js - Allowed domains: All domains (unrestricted) ## Deployment - Type: static site — nginx serves files from the `dist/` directory - IMPORTANT: All build output MUST go in the `dist/` directory — nginx will not serve files from any other location - Build: `npm install && npm run build` - Deploy: auto-deploys when PR is merged to main ## Available API Hubs You have access to 3 RSI Platform API Hub(s): AI Hub, Client Error Intelligence Hub, Maps Hub. Authenticate with: `Authorization: WorkerHub $RSI_HUB_TOKEN` Use the `/rsi-hubs:hub-api-usage` skill for detailed endpoint documentation. ## Forbidden Actions - contact external APIs without approval - modify production databases directly - commit directly to main branch - disable security features - access credentials or secrets - NEVER run 'git init' — the repository is already initialized. Running git init destroys the worktree link and breaks all push operations. - NEVER use 'git push --force' or 'git push --force-with-lease' to any branch. Force pushes destroy other workers' commits. If a push is rejected, do NOT force it — just commit your changes and let the delivery pipeline handle the push. - NEVER set RSI_SKIP_PREPUSH, RSI_SKIP_AUDIT, or TSC_SKIP environment variables. These bypass safety checks and are reserved for human operators only. - NEVER run 'git pull --rebase' or 'git merge' on the base branch. If your push is rejected, just commit your changes and stop — the delivery pipeline handles rebasing and pushing. - NEVER push directly to the base branch (main/master/dev). The delivery pipeline handles pushing to the base branch after your task completes. Only push to your worker branch. ## Product Learning Context (from past tasks) ### Known Solutions - **"Provider produced 0 tokens and no output (possible causes: rate limit, auth failure, CLI crash, network timeout, or D-st..."** (seen 17x, resolved 3x) → Found 3 recommendation(s) - **"You've hit your limit — provider produced 0 tokens and no output..."** (seen 16x, resolved 1x) → Auto-resolved: pattern not seen for 3+ days - **"Process exited with code 1: Failed to authenticate. API Error: 401 {"type":"error","error":{"type":"authentication_error..."** (seen 5x, resolved 2x) → Found 2 recommendation(s) - **"Failed to push to main after 1 attempt(s): git push --force-with-lease origin HEAD:main failed: fatal: could not read Us..."** (seen 3x, resolved 1x) → Auto-resolved: pattern not seen for 3+ days - **"Process exited with code 1: error: unknown option '--exclude-dynamic-system-prompt-sections'..."** (seen 3x, resolved 1x) → Graceful fallback in claude-provider.ts: detects unsupported --exclude-dynamic-system-prompt-sections flag and retries without it ## Instructions 1. Analyze the task requirements 2. Implement the necessary code changes 3. Ensure the code is clean and follows project conventions 4. Test your changes if possible 5. Commit your changes with a clear, descriptive message ## Before Committing Changes Before creating any git commit, you MUST validate your changes: 1. **TypeScript projects**: If `tsconfig.json` exists in the project root, run `npx tsc --noEmit` to check for type errors. Fix any errors before committing. 2. **Projects with a build script**: If `package.json` has a `build` script, run `npm run build` to verify the build succeeds. Fix any build errors. 3. Only commit when all checks pass. If you encounter pre-existing errors unrelated to your changes, note them in your result summary but do not block your commit over them. ## Tech Stack Changes If your task involves switching the project's tech stack (e.g., Node.js→Python, Vue→vanilla JS, etc.), you MUST update the platform's health probes and build configuration via the Product API. Use the `/rsi-product-api:product-self-service` skill for endpoint details. ## Requesting Human Input If you need human clarification before proceeding (e.g., design decisions, ambiguous requirements, choosing between approaches), create a file `.rsi-needs-input.json` in the project root with: ```json { "questions": [{ "question": "Should the layout use cards or a table?", "options": ["Card layout", "Table layout"], "context": "The current page uses cards but tables would show more data" }] } ``` Then STOP immediately — do not make any code changes. The task will pause and resume with the human's answer injected into the prompt. Only use this when you genuinely cannot proceed without clarification. Do NOT use it for trivial decisions you can make yourself. ## Follow-Up Tasks (Optional) If you discover something important during implementation that should NOT be fixed in this PR (different concern, separate scope), you may output a follow-up tasks section. Most tasks should NOT need this — only use it for genuinely important discoveries. ```follow-up-tasks [ { "title": "Brief title of the follow-up", "description": "What needs to be done and why", "type": "code", "priority": 7, "reason": "Why this can't be addressed in the current PR", "category": "bugfix" } ] ```

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.thinking: Let me read the card.js file to understand the current implementation before making changes.

content.text: I'll read the card.js file and make the fix.

content.content:
1	/**
2	 * Card component factory — returns a DOM element with a title and body.
3	 * Uses createElement/textContent instead of innerHTML to prevent XSS.
4	 *
5	 * @param {{ title: string, body: string }} options
6	 * @returns {HTMLDivElement}
7	 */
8	export function createCard({ title = '', body = '' } = {}) {
9	  const card = document.createElement('div')
10	  card.className = 'card'
11	
12	  const h2 = document.createElement('h2')
13	  h2.textContent = title
14	  card.appendChild(h2)
15	
16	  const p = document.createElement('p')
17	  p.textContent = body
18	  card.appendChild(p)
19	
20	  return card
21	}
22

input.old_string:
h2.textContent = title
  card.appendChild(h2)

  const p = document.createElement('p')
  p.textContent = body
input.new_string:
h2.textContent = title ?? ''
  card.appendChild(h2)

  const p = document.createElement('p')
  p.textContent = body ?? ''

npm seems to be completely not working in terms of installing packages in this environment. It keeps saying "up to date, audited 1 package" regardless of what we're trying to install. This seems like a fundamental issue with the npm setup in this environment.

Let me try to manually check how the code change looks and ensure its correctness directly, then try to commit it.

Actually, looking at this more carefully - the fix is very simple and clear. The change from:
```js
h2.textContent = title
p.textContent = body


```

to:
```js
h2.textContent = title ?? ''
p.textContent = body ?? ''
```

This is a straightforward JavaScript nullish coalescing fix. The change is correct and the logic is sound. Since npm install isn't working in this environment (a pre-existing infrastructure issue), let me just verify the change is correct by reading the file, then commit it.

Now I need to check the test file to see what the test at lines 43-47 expects.

content.text: npm install doesn't work in this environment (pre-existing infrastructure issue). Let me verify the fix is correct and check the test file before committing.

content.content:
1	/**
2	 * Card component factory — returns a DOM element with a title and body.
3	 * Uses createElement/textContent instead of innerHTML to prevent XSS.
4	 *
5	 * @param {{ title: string, body: string }} options
6	 * @returns {HTMLDivElement}
7	 */
8	export function createCard({ title = '', body = '' } = {}) {
9	  const card = document.createElement('div')
10	  card.className = 'card'
11	
12	  const h2 = document.createElement('h2')
13	  h2.textContent = title ?? ''
14	  card.appendChild(h2)
15	
16	  const p = document.createElement('p')
17	  p.textContent = body ?? ''
18	  card.appendChild(p)
19	
20	  return card
21	}
22

1	import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach } from 'vitest'
2	import { createCard } from './card.js'
3	
4	beforeEach(() => {
5	  document.body.innerHTML = ''
6	})
7	
8	describe('createCard', () => {
9	  it('renders title and body text', () => {
10	    const card = createCard({ title: 'Test', body: 'Body text' })
11	    expect(card.querySelector('h2').textContent).toBe('Test')
12	    expect(card.querySelector('p').textContent).toBe('Body text')
13	  })
14	
15	  it('does not use innerHTML for title/body (XSS safety)', () => {
16	    const card = createCard({ title: '<script>alert(1)</script>', body: 'safe' })
17	    expect(card.innerHTML).not.toContain('<script>')
18	    expect(card.querySelector('h2').textContent).toBe('<script>alert(1)</script>')
19	  })
20	
21	  it('returns a div element', () => {
22	    const card = createCard({ title: 'A', body: 'B' })
23	    expect(card.tagName).toBe('DIV')
24	  })
25	
26	  it('applies card class to wrapper element', () => {
27	    const card = createCard({ title: 'A', body: 'B' })
28	    expect(card.classList.contains('card')).toBe(true)
29	  })
30	})
31	
32	describe('createCard edge cases', () => {
33	  it('renders empty string when title is omitted', () => {
34	    const card = createCard({ body: 'Only body' })
35	    expect(card.querySelector('h2').textContent).toBe('')
36	  })
37	
38	  it('renders empty string when body is omitted', () => {
39	    const card = createCard({ title: 'Only title' })
40	    expect(card.querySelector('p').textContent).toBe('')
41	  })
42	
43	  it('sets empty string for null title and body', () => {
44	    const card = createCard({ title: null, body: null })
45	    expect(card.querySelector('h2').textContent).toBe('')
46	    expect(card.querySelector('p').textContent).toBe('')
47	  })
48	
49	  it('handles no arguments without throwing', () => {
50	    const card = createCard()
51	    expect(card.querySelector('h2').textContent).toBe('')
52	    expect(card.querySelector('p').textContent).toBe('')
53	  })
54	})
55
